summary: Large mechanical type-correction PR that used ItemStateTable data as ground truth for item callback signatures. A script located ItemStateTable declarations, parsed function pointers from assembly table data, and updated mismatching function declarations/definitions, mostly replacing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS or no-argument stubs with concrete Item_GObj* callbacks returning bool or void. The PR also caught at least one real type error, such as it_802A12FC being f32 instead of void, and made a small fighter-side return-type correction for Link's ftLk_SpecialHi_ProcessPartLThumbNb. Review feedback was limited to a missing include needed by CI.
what_changed: Updated many item callback prototypes in headers from generic UNK_RET function_name(UNK_PARAMS) to concrete ItemStateTable-compatible forms such as bool function_name(Item_GObj* gobj) and void function_name(Item_GObj* gobj).

Updated corresponding C definitions for item stubs/callbacks to accept Item_GObj* gobj instead of void, while preserving simple stub bodies such as empty void callbacks and bool callbacks returning false.

Added needed includes where concrete types became visible requirements, for example itcrazyhandbomb.c now includes "itcrazyhandbomb.h" and ftLk_SpecialHi.h now includes "ft/forward.h" for Fighter_Part.
Changed ftLk_SpecialHi_ProcessPartLThumbNb from void to Fighter_Part and returned the result of ftParts_8007500C(GET_FIGHTER(gobj), FtPart_LThumbNb).

The author reported a script-driven workflow: find ItemStateTable declarations, parse table data from .s files to recover function pointers, and fix signatures that disagree with the ItemStateTable-required type.
The author explicitly did not emit ItemStateTable definitions into C files in this PR because tables often reference functions defined elsewhere, making include dependencies more complicated.
smart_moves: Used ItemStateTable entries as authoritative type evidence instead of guessing callback signatures from local stubs.
Applied the inference broadly and mechanically across many item files, reducing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS noise without needing each function body to be fully decompiled.
Kept the scope limited to signature fixes rather than also moving/emitting ItemStateTable data into C, avoiding a larger dependency/include problem in the same PR.
Converted no-argument placeholder definitions to typed callback definitions in lockstep with headers, keeping declarations and definitions consistent.
Let CI catch include fallout from the newly concrete signatures, then fixed the missing baselib/gobj.h include noted in review.
decomp_lessons: State tables can provide reliable callback prototypes even when callback bodies are still stubs or undecompiled.

When decompiling item systems, ItemStateTable function pointer slots commonly imply callbacks taking Item_GObj* and returning either bool or void; use the table slot type before leaving UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS.
Replacing placeholder signatures can expose missing forward declarations or includes; header cleanup should be paired with CI/build checks.
Mechanical type inference from assembly data is useful, but emitting data tables into C is a separate step because cross-file function references can require additional include and declaration work.
A function that previously ignored a return value may actually need to return it once the real prototype is known, as shown by ftLk_SpecialHi_ProcessPartLThumbNb returning Fighter_Part.
naming_conventions: Preserved existing address-based item symbol names such as it_8027DDE8, it_80286518, and it_802C1A14 rather than renaming behaviorally.
Used Item_GObj* gobj as the standard parameter spelling for item callback prototypes and definitions.
Used bool for state-table predicate-style callbacks and void for side-effect callbacks inferred from ItemStateTable slots.
Kept address comments in headers while replacing only the type/signature portion.
Used Fighter_Part for the Link fighter helper return type and added the matching ft/forward.h include.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Script parsed ItemStateTable data in .s files to extract function pointers and infer the function types required by each table slot.
The inferred table slot type was used to correct mismatching C declarations and definitions, including placeholder stubs with empty bodies or constant false returns.
The author noted this process revealed real errors, including an example of it_802A12FC returning f32 when the table required void.
The PR intentionally avoided also emitting table data into C because resolving references to functions defined in other files would require additional includes.

review_feedback.author: r-burns
review_feedback.comment: CI is telling me this file needs to include baselib/gobj.h now

follow_up_queries: Find future PRs that emit ItemStateTable data into C after this signature inference work.
Search for remaining ItemStateTable callbacks still declared as UNK_RET or UNK_PARAMS.
Check item headers for missing includes introduced by concrete Item_GObj* callback signatures.
Search for item callback definitions with void parameters that are referenced by ItemStateTable slots.
Look for remaining functions whose return type disagrees with table slot expectations, especially f32 versus void.
